title: Opus 4.8 vs GPT 5.6 Sol - Mobile AAB

app.name: Giri Brew Companion
features: two-way ratio calculator with strength and cup helper
guided staged brew timer with start, pause, skip, reset, and haptics
persistent brew log with add, edit, delete, ratings, tags, and notes
settings for default method, strength, cup size, theme, and clear-all-data

artifact.signer: CN=Giri Coffee Works, OU=Kriya Lab, O=Giri Coffee Works, L=Bengaluru, ST=Karnataka, C=IN

comparison.basis: Objective artifact audit covering deployability, specification coverage, correctness, code quality, offline/privacy posture, UX evidence, and production readiness.
comparison.interpretation: GPT 5.6 Sol leads through broader automated test coverage, cleaner code separation, and stronger release-readiness evidence. Both arms produced signed, locally verified AABs.

token_comparison.basis: new tokens (non-cached input + output) for the build phase; see each arm's token_usage
token_comparison.verdict: No clean efficiency winner is claimed. The two figures come from different harnesses (Claude Code interactive session vs Codex CLI wrapper) with different cache accounting; the GPT run hit an interrupt/transcript loop and the Opus session also did lab-authoring/grading beyond the build. Both build-phase costs are the same order of magnitude (~285k-433k new tokens).

limitations: Token figures are cross-harness and not perfectly scope-matched, so no token-efficiency or cost winner is declared; both are the same order of magnitude.
Neither arm has a recorded emulator-based usability or accessibility evaluation.

independent_verification.verified_by: Claude Code, re-run against the on-disk artifacts on 2026-07-15

checks.secrets_git_ignored: key.properties and *.jks confirmed git-ignored for both arms; both app folders git-ignored in hub

token_usage.definition: new_tokens = non-cached input + output (fresh work, excludes cache reads)
token_usage.note: Total wire tokens (8.9M) are inflated by ~8.6M cached-input reads from the interrupted transcript loop; the 285,373 new-token figure is the fresh-compute cost.

gpt56sol.run_note: The GPT wrapper was interrupted after a repeated verbose transcript loop; it had already produced a signed AAB. The final recorded artifact below was rebuilt and verified independently after interruption.

artifact.signer: CN=Filter Brew Companion Upload, OU=GPT 5.6 Sol Lab, O=Offline Lab, L=Bengaluru, ST=Karnataka, C=IN

quality_gates.permissions_scan: No network/runtime permissions; AndroidX adds app-defined com.gpt56sol.filterbrew.DYNAMIC_RECEIVER_NOT_EXPORTED_PERMISSION in merged manifest.

opus48.served_model_verified: claude-opus-4-8 recorded on all 254 assistant turns of the build session transcript 7979a130
opus48.artifact_note: Opus-side AAB is complete and locally verified: file exists, SHA-256 recorded, jarsigner reports jar verified, and expected bundle entries/ABIs are present.

token_usage.definition: new_tokens = non-cached input (base + cache-creation) + output; same fresh-work basis as the GPT figure
token_usage.build_phase_note: Cumulative new tokens through the first signed .aab (flutter build appbundle). This is the scope-matched comparator to the GPT 285,373.
token_usage.source: Claude Code build-session transcript 7979a130-...jsonl (254 assistant turns, all model=claude-opus-4-8)
token_usage.note: The full session also authored the lab (BRIEF/RUBRIC/scoring), ran the independent grading workflow, verified with jarsigner, and pushed repos — so full_session tokens are NOT app-build-only. Use build_phase_new_tokens for a like-for-like build comparison.
